extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3 (priority 50), whose condition is the conjunction of 12 inputs including asthma. rec_drug_b is set true only by r4 (priority 50), whose condition is asthma. Because r3's firing condition entails asthma=true, any input that fires r3 also fires r4. No higher-priority or conflicting rule resets either output (defaults apply only to unset outputs, and the only rules touching rec_drug_a/rec_drug_b assign true). Therefore the assignment where all 12 conjuncts of r3 are true (asthma, hypertension, low_ejection_fraction, qt_prolongation, diabetes, pregnancy, peptic_ulcer, hepatic_impairment, bradycardia, renal_impairment, elderly, prior_gi_bleed) makes both rec_drug_a (via r3) and rec_drug_b (via r4) true, violating the mutual-exclusion property. The three remaining inputs (polypharmacy, seizure_history, heart_failure) affect only rec_drug_c and are set false for a minimal witness.
